summary: The problem of whether every countably infinite graph has an unfriendly partition into two sets remains open. Significant partial results have appeared since 2007: the conjecture has been confirmed for rayless graphs, for graphs not containing a subdivision of an infinite clique, for line graphs of any cardinality, and most recently (2024) for all countable graphs without alternating rays — rays that pass through infinitely many vertices of both finite and infinite degree. The difficult case of countable graphs that do admit alternating rays is still unresolved.

since_posted.title: Unfriendly or weakly unfriendly partitions of graphs
since_posted.claim: Proves an equivalence: the existence of unfriendly partitions for all countable graphs is equivalent to their existence for all countable graphs lacking induced subgraphs of infinite minimum degree, and establishes omega_n-unfriendly partitions for graphs with infinite minimum degree.

since_posted.title: List majority edge-colorings of graphs
since_posted.authors: Rafał Kalinowski, Monika Pilśniak, Marcin Stawiski
since_posted.claim: Proves the Unfriendly Partition Conjecture for line graphs of any cardinality by showing they admit majority vertex-colorings from lists of size 2.

since_posted.title: Unfriendly partitions when avoiding vertices of finite degree
since_posted.claim: Shows that the minimum cardinality of a graph (without vertices of finite degree) that admits no unfriendly partition cannot be determined in ZFC, establishing a set-theoretic independence result.

since_posted.title: Remarks on the countable case of the Unfriendly Partition Problem
since_posted.claim: Proves that every countable graph without alternating rays admits an unfriendly partition, strictly generalizing results of Aharoni-Milner-Prikry and Bruhn-Diestel-Georgakopoulos-Sprüssel.

search_queries: unfriendly partition countably infinite graph conjecture recent results
unfriendly partitions graph theory arXiv 2007 2025 countable
Cowan Emerson conjecture unfriendly partition solved counterexample
Aharoni Milner Prikry unfriendly partition infinite graph progress
notes: The Bruhn-Diestel-Georgakopoulos-Sprüssel (2010) paper on rayless graphs and the Berger (2017) paper on graphs without a subdivision of an infinite clique are both cited in verified sources as important post-2007 partial results, but their direct URLs could not be individually fetched and confirmed in this review, so they are not listed in since_posted. The published journal version of the line graphs paper (Combinatorica 45, 2025, DOI 10.1007/s00493-024-00131-1) was inaccessible via Springer; the arXiv preprint (2312.00922) was verified instead. The December 2024 paper (2412.14151) explicitly states the problem remains open for countable graphs that do admit alternating rays.
